title: Cricket in India
description: A cricket round with India-focused moments, captains, tournaments, venues, and iconic achievements.

questions.prompt: Who captained India to the 1983 Cricket World Cup title?
questions.hint: He was a great all-rounder from Haryana.

questions.prompt: Who captained India to the 2011 Cricket World Cup title?
questions.hint: He finished the final with a famous six.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer is known as the Master Blaster?
questions.hint: He made his debut as a teenager in 1989.

questions.prompt: Which stadium in Ahmedabad is the largest cricket stadium in India?
questions.hint: It is named after a famous Indian leader.

questions.prompt: Which Indian bowler took all 10 wickets in a Test innings against Pakistan in 1999?
questions.hint: He was nicknamed Jumbo.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer is called the Prince of Kolkata or Dada?
questions.hint: He later became BCCI president.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian captain to win the T20 World Cup?
questions.hint: It happened in 2007.

questions.prompt: Which Indian batter scored the first double century in men's ODI cricket?
questions.hint: It happened against South Africa in Gwalior.

questions.prompt: Which IPL team is based in Chennai?
questions.hint: Its captain for many years was M. S. Dhoni.

questions.prompt: Which IPL team is based in Mumbai?
questions.hint: It has won the title multiple times.

questions.prompt: Who is known as the Wall of Indian cricket?
questions.hint: He later coached India.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer is famously known as the Hitman?
questions.hint: He has scored multiple ODI double hundreds.

questions.prompt: Who led India in the famous 2001 Kolkata Test comeback against Australia?
questions.hint: He was known for waving his shirt at Lord's later that year.

questions.prompt: Which two Indian batters had the historic partnership at Eden Gardens in 2001 against Australia?
questions.hint: One was V. V. S. Laxman.
questions.answer: V. V. S. Laxman and Rahul Dravid

questions.prompt: What is the full form of IPL?
questions.hint: It is India's most famous franchise T20 tournament.

questions.prompt: Which Indian fast bowler was known as the Sultan of Swing?
questions.hint: His first name is Zaheer.

questions.prompt: Which Indian spinner is known for bowling the carrom ball?
questions.hint: His surname is Ashwin.

questions.prompt: Who was the first Indian to score a triple century in Test cricket?
questions.hint: He was from Multan fame's generation and from Punjab.

questions.prompt: Which Indian batter is known as Mr. 360 in modern T20 circles?
questions.hint: His first name is Suryakumar.

questions.prompt: Which Indian city is home to Eden Gardens?
questions.hint: It is the capital of West Bengal.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer is known as King Kohli?
questions.hint: He captained India in all formats.

questions.prompt: Which trophy is awarded to the winner of the Ranji tournament?
questions.hint: It is India's premier domestic first-class competition.

questions.prompt: Which Indian all-rounder was called the Haryana Hurricane?
questions.hint: He captained the 1983 World Cup team.

questions.prompt: Who hit the winning six for India in the 2011 World Cup final?
questions.hint: He was India's captain in that match.

questions.prompt: Who is known as the Little Master of Indian cricket from an earlier generation?
questions.hint: His surname is Gavaskar.

questions.prompt: Which Indian all-rounder was nicknamed Yuvi?
questions.hint: He was Player of the Tournament in the 2011 World Cup.

questions.prompt: Which Indian spinner from Tamil Nadu was known as Venkatapathy in full name and played in the 1983 World Cup final?
questions.hint: His surname is Raju.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer scored 183 against Sri Lanka in the 2003 World Cup?
questions.hint: He opened the innings with a fearless style.

questions.prompt: Which Indian batter made 281 in the Kolkata Test against Australia in 2001?
questions.hint: His initials are V. V. S.

questions.prompt: What does LBW stand for in cricket?
questions.hint: It refers to a batter being out with the pad in front.

questions.prompt: Which Indian city is home to the Wankhede Stadium?
questions.hint: It is Maharashtra's capital.

questions.prompt: Which Indian city is home to M. A. Chidambaram Stadium?
questions.hint: It is often called Chepauk.

questions.prompt: Which city is associated with the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium?
questions.hint: It is the capital of Karnataka.

questions.prompt: Which Indian state team does the domestic giant Mumbai represent in first-class cricket?
questions.hint: Its capital is Mumbai too.

questions.prompt: Which Indian batter is known for the phrase 'The Nawab of Najafgarh'?
questions.hint: He was an explosive opener.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer has the nickname Jaddu?
questions.hint: He is a left-arm spinning all-rounder.

questions.prompt: Which Indian pace bowler was nicknamed Bhuvi?
questions.hint: His surname is Kumar.

questions.prompt: Which Indian player is famous for the Desert Storm innings in Sharjah?
questions.hint: He is the Master Blaster.

questions.prompt: Which Indian captain lifted the Champions Trophy jointly with Sri Lanka in 2002?
questions.hint: He is called Dada.

questions.prompt: Which Indian cricketer won the 1985 World Championship of Cricket Player of the Series award?
questions.hint: He later became captain of India.

questions.prompt: Which left-handed Indian opener from Delhi was nicknamed Gabbar?
questions.hint: His surname is Dhawan.

questions.prompt: Which Indian wicketkeeper was nicknamed Mahi?
questions.hint: He captained India to three major ICC titles.

questions.prompt: Which Indian spinner from the 1970s and 1980s was known as one half of the famous spin quartet along with Bedi, Chandra, and Venkataraghavan?
questions.hint: His surname is Prasanna.

questions.prompt: What is the standard number of players in a cricket team on the field?
questions.hint: Think of football plus one.

questions.prompt: Which color ball is traditionally used in day-night limited-overs cricket?
questions.hint: It is easier to see under lights than the red one.

questions.prompt: Which Indian city hosted the 2011 World Cup final that India won?
questions.hint: The match was at Wankhede Stadium.

questions.prompt: Which award is given to the top-performing player in an IPL season?
questions.hint: It is named after the most valuable player concept.

questions.prompt: Which Indian player was called the Turbanator?
questions.hint: He was an off-spinner from Punjab.

questions.prompt: Which Indian batter was known as Dashing and was famous for his cover drives, with the initials M. A. K.?
questions.hint: His first name is Mohammad.

questions.prompt: Which Indian pacer took a hat-trick in the 2019 World Cup against Afghanistan?
questions.hint: His surname is Shami.
